data.body:
BRAINSTORM CORRECTION — RETRACTION of unconfirmed 'decisions'.

User feedback: I (the agent) jumped from research straight to recording dependency adoptions + critique refinements as DECISIONS without discussing tradeoffs/alternatives or getting user confirmation, and without vetting imports against the project bar.

RETRACTED — the following are NOT decisions; reopened as OPTIONS pending explicit build-vs-import discussion:
- ALL the 'IMPORT' verdicts in decision log 5/N (mcp client, ruamel.yaml, python-frontmatter, jsonsubschema, jsonschema+referencing, Ajv2020, pathspec). These were a subagent's verdicts, not user-confirmed, and were not checked against the import bar.
- The 8 'ADOPTED REFINEMENTS' batch-listed in decision log 4/N. These were an adversarial-critique subagent's recommendations; treat each as an OPEN proposal, not an adopted decision.

PROJECT IMPORT BAR (user, explicit): we will NOT import anything that is not BOTH (a) popular on GitHub AND (b) actively maintained. For every capability, BUILD and the relevant STANDARD/PATTERN remain alternatives on the table. The four framings — reinvention, established patterns, standards conformance, peer-handled gotchas — are INPUTS that frame each build-vs-import-vs-conform decision, not an after-the-fact checklist.

STILL STANDING (user-confirmed, NOT retracted): Q1 decomposed-triple primitive; Q2 contract home = front-matter + schema-registry names; Q3 type-aware-expr-validation + inspector (validation DEPTH still open); Q4 mcp_call = external servers; Q5 MCP two-tier allowlist+secrets; Q6 write-back auto-detect editable checkout; Q7 cached manifest+refresh; Q8a execution_mode fixed on prompt; Q8b v1 = prompt model+contracts, MCP+deep-typecheck = v1.1.

NEXT: triage every candidate dependency (+ alternatives) against the import bar with hard metrics; then decide build-vs-import per capability, one at a time, framed by standards/patterns/gotchas. No tickets written yet.
